heading: Prohibition on management of depot employees by end strength
label: 2472 Prohibition on management of depot employees by end strength
text: The civilian employees of the Department of Defense, including the civilian employees of the military departments and the Defense Agencies, who perform, or are involved in the performance of, depot-level maintenance and repair workloads may not be managed on the basis of any constraint or limitation in terms of man years, end strength, full-time equivalent positions, or maximum number of employees. Such employees shall be managed solely on the basis of the available workload and the funds made available for such depot-level maintenance and repair.
notes: CodificationThe text of , which was transferred to this section and redesignated subsec. (a) by , was based on , , ; , , .section 2466(b) of this titlePub. L. 104–106, § 312(b)Pub. L. 102–190, div. A, title III, § 314(a)(1)105 Stat. 1336Pub. L. 103–337, div. A, title III, § 332(b)108 Stat. 2715Dec. 5, 1991Oct. 5, 1994

Amendments2004— substituted “Prohibition on management of depot employees by end strength” for “Management of depot employees” in section catchline, struck out subsec. (a) designation and heading before “The civilian”, and struck out heading and text of subsec. (b). Text read as follows: “Not later than December 1 of each fiscal year, the Secretary of Defense shall submit to the Committee on Armed Services of the Senate and the Committee on Armed Services of the House of Representatives a report on the number of employees employed and expected to be employed by the Department of Defense during that fiscal year to perform depot-level maintenance and repair of materiel. The report shall indicate whether that number is sufficient to perform the depot-level maintenance and repair functions for which funds are expected to be provided for that fiscal year for performance by Department of Defense employees.”Pub. L. 108–3751999—Subsec. (b). substituted “and the Committee on Armed Services” for “and the Committee on National Security”.Pub. L. 106–651997—Subsec. (a). inserted first sentence and struck out former first sentence which read as follows: “The civilian employees of the Department of Defense involved in the depot-level maintenance and repair of materiel may not be managed on the basis of any end-strength constraint or limitation on the number of such employees who may be employed on the last day of a fiscal year.”Pub. L. 105–851996—Subsec. (a). , renumbered as subsec. (a) of this section.Pub. L. 104–106, § 312(b)section 2466(b) of this title

Submission of Initial Report, , , required the report under subsec. (b) of this section for fiscal year 1996 to be submitted not later than .Pub. L. 104–106, div. A, title III, § 312(c)110 Stat. 250Feb. 10, 1996Mar. 15, 1996
